titleStmt.title: SII 17.455: original edition by K. G. Krishnan
No. 455. (A.R. No. 424 of 1904.) VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.

summary.p: On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
On palæographical considerations, the king may be identified with Rājarāja III.

This inscription records the gift of jewels in gold and silver such as tiruneṟṟimālai (forehead plate) bāhu-valaiyam (armlet), tirukkaichchari (bracelet), tirumulaittaḍam (breast plate), tiruvāram (necklace), udarabandhanam (belt), tiruppiṟai (crescent), etc., to god Maṇavāḷa-nāyaṉār and the goddess Nāchchiyār of Tirumaṟaikkāḍu by Periyāṉ Śelkalāṇḍāṉ of Nelvēli, a merchant of Kulōttuṅgaśōḻappaṭṭiṉam. Also gives the weight of gold and silver for these jewels.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīrājarājatevaṟku yāṇṭu 10 6 nSuperfluous.vatiṉ Etirāmāṇṭu tirumaṟaikkāṭṭu maṇavāḷa
nāyaṉāṟkum nācciyāṟkum cāttiyaruḷak kulottuṅkacoThis and the corresponding sections in the following lines are built in.ḻapaṭṭiṉattu viyāpāri nelveli yuṭaiyāṉ pe
riyāṉ celkalāṇṭāṉ ceyvitta tiruneṟṟimālai tiruvāram Utarapentaṉam tiruppiṟai pākuvalaiyam Ira
ṇṭum tirukkaiccari Iraṇṭum māṉu(m) maḻuvukkum nācciyāṟku tiruvapicekam tiruneṟṟi mālai tiruvāram tiru
mulaittaṭattukku muṭpaṭa poṉ Aimpatteḻu kaḻañcemukkāle mañcāṭiyu meḻu mā(k) kuccai
kku Iṭṭa veḷḷi Eṇpattaiṅ kaḻañcaraiye mañcāṭiyum nālu mā
Digital edition of SII 17.455 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
